old_claim.note: Barcodes provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: Barcode labels and scanners provide the automatic-identification mechanism for warehouse receiving, picking, routing, inventory, and traceability workflows.

source_shape.source_locator: GS1 US describes the UPC barcode scan as a pivotal moment for supply-chain automation and says barcodes continue to support real-time inventory and traceability.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source ties barcode scanning to supply-chain automation, inventory visibility, and traceability.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The warehouse node is specifically barcode-enabled automation, so automatic identification is the enabling mechanism rather than generic logistics context.

invariant_preserved_or_changed: Preserved: barcodes remains an enabling edge, now with review-level source support.
would_reject_if: The node were narrowed to non-barcode warehouse automation such as RFID-only or vision-only workflows.
A stronger source showed warehouse barcode automation did not use barcode labels or scanners for inventory identification.
A future node split separated barcode scanning hardware from warehouse workflow automation.
short_reason: Barcode scanning is the automatic-identification mechanism.
